23: 	ErrWeekStartDate      = errors.New("start must use YYYY-MM-DD")
24: )
25: 
26: type PlanService struct {
27: 	plans *repositories.PlanRepository
28: }
29: 
30: func NewPlanService(plans *repositories.PlanRepository) *PlanService {
31: 	return &PlanService{plans: plans}
32: }
33: 
34: func (s *PlanService) CreateProposal(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PlanProposalRequest) (dto.ProposalResponse, error) {
35: 	if err := validatePlanProposal(req); err != nil {
36: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
37: 	}
38: 	if req.Source == "" {
39: 		req.Source = "ai"
40: 	}
41: 	if req.ProposalVersion == 0 {
42: 		req.ProposalVersion = 1
43: 	}
44: 	if req.EndDate == "" {
45: 		req.EndDate = req.StartDate
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	plan, err := s.plans.CreateProposal(ctx, userID, req)
49: 	if err != nil {
50: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return buildProposalResponse([]repositories.StoredPlan{plan}), nil
54: }
55: 
56: func (s *PlanService) AcceptProposal(ctx context.Context, userID, planID string) (dto.ProposalResponse, error) {
57: 	plan, err := s.plans.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
58: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
59: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrInvalidPlanStatus
60: 	}
61: 	if err != nil {
62: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
63: 	}
64: 	if plan.Status != "proposal" {
65: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrPlanNotProposal
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	updated, err := s.plans.UpdateStatus(ctx, userID, planID, "accepted", "")
69: 	if err != nil {
70: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	return buildProposalResponse([]repositories.StoredPlan{updated}), nil
74: }
75: 
76: func (s *PlanService) DeclineProposal(ctx context.Context, userID, planID, reason string) (dto.ProposalResponse, error) {
77: 	plan, err := s.plans.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
78: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
79: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrInvalidPlanStatus
80: 	}
81: 	if err != nil {
82: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
83: 	}
84: 	if plan.Status != "proposal" {
85: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrPlanNotProposal
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	note := ""
89: 	if strings.TrimSpace(reason) != "" {
90: 		note = "decline_reason: " + strings.TrimSpace(reason)
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	updated, err := s.plans.UpdateStatus(ctx, userID, planID, "declined", note)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	return buildProposalResponse([]repositories.StoredPlan{updated}), nil
99: }
100: 
101: func (s *PlanService) GetWeekPlan(ctx context.Context, userID, startDate string) (dto.WeekPlanResponse, error) {
102: 	start, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", startDate)
103: 	if err != nil {
104: 		return dto.WeekPlanResponse{}, ErrWeekStartDate
105: 	}
106: 	endDate := start.AddDate(0, 0, 6).Format("2006-01-02")
107: 	plans, err := s.plans.ListAcceptedPlansForRange(ctx, userID, startDate, endDate)
108: 	if err != nil {
109: 		return dto.WeekPlanResponse{}, err
110: 	}
111: 	return buildWeekPlanResponse(plans, startDate, endDate), nil
112: }
113: 
114: func validatePlanProposal(req dto.PlanProposalRequest) error {
115: 	switch req.PeriodType {
116: 	case "day", "week", "month":
117: 	default:
118: 		return ErrInvalidPlanType
119: 	}
120: 	if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.StartDate); err != nil {
121: 		return ErrInvalidStartDate
122: 	}
123: 	if req.EndDate != "" {
124: 		if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.EndDate); err != nil {
125: 			return ErrInvalidEndDate
126: 		}
127: 	}
128: 	if len(req.Meals) == 0 {
129: 		return ErrInvalidPlanMeals
130: 	}
131: 	for _, meal := range req.Meals {
132: 		switch meal.MealSection {
133: 		case "breakfast", "lunch", "dinner", "snacks":
134: 		default:
135: 			return ErrInvalidMealSection
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 	return nil
139: }
140: 
141: func buildProposalResponse(plans []repositories.StoredPlan) dto.ProposalResponse {
142: 	if len(plans) == 0 {
143: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}
144: 	}
145: 	week := buildWeekPlanResponse(plans, plans[0].StartDate, plans[0].EndDate)
146: 	if len(week.Plans) == 0 {
147: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}
148: 	}
149: 	return dto.ProposalResponse{
150: 		Plan:       week.Plans[0],
151: 		Days:       week.Days,
152: 		WeekTotals: week.WeekTotals,
153: 	}
154: }
155: 
156: func buildWeekPlanResponse(plans []repositories.StoredPlan, startDate, endDate string) dto.WeekPlanResponse {
157: 	planSummaries := make([]dto.PlanSummaryResponse, 0, len(plans))
158: 	allMeals := make([]repositories.StoredPlanMeal, 0)
159: 	for _, plan := range plans {
160: 		planSummaries = append(planSummaries, dto.PlanSummaryResponse{
161: 			ID:               plan.ID,
162: 			PeriodType:       plan.PeriodType,
163: 			StartDate:        plan.StartDate,
164: 			EndDate:          plan.EndDate,
165: 			Status:           plan.Status,
166: 			Source:           plan.Source,
167: 			ProposalVersion:  plan.ProposalVersion,
168: 			AICostCentsTotal: plan.AICostCentsTotal,
169: 			Notes:            plan.Notes,
170: 		})
171: 		allMeals = append(allMeals, plan.Meals...)
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	sort.Slice(allMeals, func(i, j int) bool {
175: 		if allMeals[i].ScheduledDate == allMeals[j].ScheduledDate {
176: 			return allMeals[i].MealSection < allMeals[j].MealSection
177: 		}
178: 		return allMeals[i].ScheduledDate < allMeals[j].ScheduledDate
179: 	})
180: 
181: 	days := make([]dto.PlanDayResponse, 0)
182: 	weeklyTotals := dto.RecipeMacrosResponse{}
183: 	mealIndex := map[string]repositories.StoredPlanMeal{}
184: 	for _, meal := range allMeals {
185: 		mealIndex[meal.ScheduledDate+"|"+meal.MealSection] = meal
186: 		weeklyTotals.Calories += meal.Calories
187: 		weeklyTotals.ProteinG += meal.ProteinG
188: 		weeklyTotals.CarbsG += meal.CarbsG
189: 		weeklyTotals.FatG += meal.FatG
190: 	}
191: 
192: 	start, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", startDate)
193: 	if err != nil {
194: 		start = deriveStartDate(plans, startDate)
195: 	}
196: 	end, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", endDate)
197: 	if err != nil {
198: 		end = deriveEndDate(plans, endDate, start)
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	for d := start; !d.After(end); d = d.AddDate(0, 0, 1) {
202: 		date := d.Format("2006-01-02")
203: 		day := dto.PlanDayResponse{Date: date}
204: 		for _, section := range []string{"breakfast", "lunch", "dinner", "snacks"} {
205: 			meal, ok := mealIndex[date+"|"+section]
206: 			if !ok {
207: 				continue
208: 			}
209: 			res := mealToDTO(meal)
210: 			switch section {
211: 			case "breakfast":
212: 				day.Sections.Breakfast = &res
213: 			case "lunch":
214: 				day.Sections.Lunch = &res
215: 			case "dinner":
216: 				day.Sections.Dinner = &res
217: 			case "snacks":
218: 				day.Sections.Snacks = &res
219: 			}
220: 			day.Totals.Calories += meal.Calories
221: 			day.Totals.ProteinG += meal.ProteinG
222: 			day.Totals.CarbsG += meal.CarbsG
223: 			day.Totals.FatG += meal.FatG
224: 		}
225: 		days = append(days, day)
226: 	}
227: 
228: 	return dto.WeekPlanResponse{Plans: planSummaries, Days: days, WeekTotals: weeklyTotals}
229: }
230: 
231: func deriveStartDate(plans []repositories.StoredPlan, fallback string) time.Time {
232: 	if fallback != "" {
233: 		if t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", fallback); err == nil {
234: 			return t
235: 		}
236: 	}
237: 	for _, plan := range plans {
238: 		if t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", plan.StartDate); err == nil {
239: 			return t
240: 		}
241: 	}
242: 	return time.Now().UTC()
243: }
244: 
245: func deriveEndDate(plans []repositories.StoredPlan, fallback string, start time.Time) time.Time {
246: 	if fallback != "" {
247: 		if t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", fallback); err == nil {
248: 			return t
249: 		}
250: 	}
251: 	for _, plan := range plans {
252: 		if t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", plan.EndDate); err == nil {
253: 			return t
254: 		}
255: 	}
256: 	return start
257: }
258: 
259: func mealToDTO(meal repositories.StoredPlanMeal) dto.PlanMealResponse {
260: 	return dto.PlanMealResponse{
261: 		ID:                 meal.ID,
262: 		RecipeID:           meal.RecipeID,
263: 		RecipeName:         meal.RecipeName,
264: 		ScheduledDate:      meal.ScheduledDate,
265: 		MealSection:        meal.MealSection,
266: 		Servings:           meal.Servings,
267: 		EstimatedCostCents: meal.EstimatedCostCents,
268: 		Macros: dto.RecipeMacrosResponse{
269: 			Calories: meal.Calories,
270: 			ProteinG: meal.ProteinG,
271: 			CarbsG:   meal.CarbsG,
272: 			FatG:     meal.FatG,
273: 		},
274: 		IsConsumed: meal.IsConsumed,
275: 		ConsumedAt: meal.ConsumedAt,
276: 	}
277: }

(End of file - total 277 lines)
</content>

)

var (
	ErrInvalidPlanType    = errors.New("periodType must be one of day, week, month")
	ErrInvalidStartDate   = errors.New("startDate must use YYYY-MM-DD")
	ErrInvalidEndDate     = errors.New("endDate must use YYYY-MM-DD")
	ErrInvalidPlanMeals   = errors.New("at least one meal is required")
	ErrInvalidMealSection = errors.New("mealSection must be one of breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks")

44: type PlanRepository struct {

47: 
48: func NewPlanRepository(db *sql.DB) *PlanRepository {

50: }
51: 
52: func (r *PlanRepository) CreateProposal(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PlanProposalRequest) (StoredPlan, error) {
53: 	planID, err := id.New("pln")
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	tx, err := r.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
59: 	if err != nil {
60: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
61: 	}
62: 	defer tx.Rollback()
63: 
64: 	_, err = tx.ExecContext(
65: 		ctx,
66: 		`INSERT INTO meal_plans (id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, notes)
67: 		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'proposal', ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
68: 		planID,
69: 		userID,
70: 		req.PeriodType,
71: 		req.StartDate,
72: 		req.EndDate,
73: 		req.Source,
74: 		req.ProposalVersion,
75: 		req.AICostCentsTotal,
76: 		req.Notes,
77: 	)
78: 	if err != nil {
79: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	for _, meal := range req.Meals {
83: 		mealID, err := id.New("pm")
84: 		if err != nil {
85: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
86: 		}
87: 
88: 		_, err = tx.ExecContext(
89: 			ctx,
90: 			`INSERT INTO plan_meals (
91: 				id, meal_plan_id, recipe_id, scheduled_date, meal_section, recipe_name,
92: 				servings, kcal, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, estimated_cost_cents
93: 			 ) VALUES (?, ?, NULLIF(?, ''), ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
94: 			mealID,
95: 			planID,
96: 			meal.RecipeID,
97: 			meal.ScheduledDate,
98: 			meal.MealSection,
99: 			meal.RecipeName,
100: 			meal.Servings,
101: 			meal.Macros.Calories,
102: 			meal.Macros.ProteinG,
103: 			meal.Macros.CarbsG,
104: 			meal.Macros.FatG,
105: 			meal.EstimatedCostCents,
106: 		)
107: 		if err != nil {
108: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
113: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	return r.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
117: }
118: 
119: func (r *PlanRepository) GetPlanByID(ctx context.Context, userID, planID string) (StoredPlan, error) {
120: 	var plan StoredPlan
121: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
122: 		ctx,
123: 		`SELECT id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, COALESCE(notes, '')
124: 		 FROM meal_plans
125: 		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
126: 		planID,
127: 		userID,
128: 	).Scan(
129: 		&plan.ID,
130: 		&plan.UserID,
131: 		&plan.PeriodType,
132: 		&plan.StartDate,
133: 		&plan.EndDate,
134: 		&plan.Status,
135: 		&plan.Source,
136: 		&plan.ProposalVersion,
137: 		&plan.AICostCentsTotal,
138: 		&plan.Notes,
139: 	)
140: 	if err != nil {
141: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
142: 	}
143: 	plan.StartDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.StartDate)
144: 	plan.EndDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.EndDate)
145: 
146: 	meals, err := r.listMealsForPlan(ctx, planID)
147: 	if err != nil {
148: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
149: 	}
150: 	plan.Meals = meals
151: 	return plan, nil
152: }
153: 
154: func (r *PlanRepository) UpdateStatus(ctx context.Context, userID, planID, status, noteSuffix string) (StoredPlan, error) {
155: 	if strings.TrimSpace(noteSuffix) == "" {
156: 		_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
157: 			ctx,
158: 			`UPDATE meal_plans SET status = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
159: 			status,
160: 			planID,
161: 			userID,
162: 		)
163: 		if err != nil {
164: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
165: 		}
166: 	} else {
167: 		_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
168: 			ctx,
169: 			`UPDATE meal_plans
170: 			 SET status = ?,
171: 			     notes = TRIM(COALESCE(notes, '') || CASE WHEN COALESCE(notes, '') = '' THEN '' ELSE ' | ' END || ?),
172: 			     updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
173: 			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
174: 			status,
175: 			noteSuffix,
176: 			planID,
177: 			userID,
178: 		)
179: 		if err != nil {
180: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	return r.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
185: }
186: 
187: func (r *PlanRepository) ListAcceptedPlansForRange(ctx context.Context, userID, startDate, endDate string) ([]StoredPlan, error) {
188: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
189: 		ctx,
190: 		`SELECT id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, COALESCE(notes, '')
191: 		 FROM meal_plans
192: 		 WHERE user_id = ? AND status = 'accepted' AND start_date <= ? AND end_date >= ?
193: 		 ORDER BY start_date ASC, created_at ASC`,
194: 		userID,
195: 		endDate,
196: 		startDate,
197: 	)
198: 	if err != nil {
199: 		return nil, err
200: 	}
201: 	defer rows.Close()
202: 
203: 	plans := make([]StoredPlan, 0)
204: 	for rows.Next() {
205: 		var plan StoredPlan
206: 		if err := rows.Scan(
207: 			&plan.ID,
208: 			&plan.UserID,
209: 			&plan.PeriodType,
210: 			&plan.StartDate,
211: 			&plan.EndDate,
212: 			&plan.Status,
213: 			&plan.Source,
214: 			&plan.ProposalVersion,
215: 			&plan.AICostCentsTotal,
216: 			&plan.Notes,
217: 		); err != nil {
218: 			return nil, err
219: 		}
220: 		plan.StartDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.StartDate)
221: 		plan.EndDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.EndDate)
222: 		plans = append(plans, plan)
223: 	}
224: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
225: 		return nil, err
226: 	}
227: 
228: 	for i := range plans {
229: 		meals, err := r.listMealsForPlanInRange(ctx, plans[i].ID, startDate, endDate)
230: 		if err != nil {
231: 			return nil, err
232: 		}
233: 		plans[i].Meals = meals
234: 	}
235: 
236: 	return plans, nil
237: }
238: 
239: func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlan(ctx context.Context, planID string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
240: 	return r.listMeals(ctx, `WHERE meal_plan_id = ?`, planID)
241: }
242: 
243: func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlanInRange(ctx context.Context, planID, startDate, endDate string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
244: 	return r.listMeals(ctx, `WHERE meal_plan_id = ? AND scheduled_date >= ? AND scheduled_date <= ?`, planID, startDate, endDate)
245: }
246: 
247: func (r *PlanRepository) listMeals(ctx context.Context, whereClause string, args ...any) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
248: 	query := fmt.Sprintf(`SELECT id, COALESCE(recipe_id, ''), recipe_name, scheduled_date, meal_section, servings,
249: 		 COALESCE(kcal, 0), COALESCE(protein_g, 0), COALESCE(carbs_g, 0), COALESCE(fat_g, 0), estimated_cost_cents,
250: 		 is_consumed, COALESCE(consumed_at, '')
251: 		 FROM plan_meals %s ORDER BY scheduled_date ASC, meal_section ASC`, whereClause)
252: 
253: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, args...)
254: 	if err != nil {
255: 		return nil, err
256: 	}
257: 	defer rows.Close()
258: 
259: 	meals := make([]StoredPlanMeal, 0)
260: 	for rows.Next() {
261: 		var meal StoredPlanMeal
262: 		var consumedInt int
263: 		if err := rows.Scan(
264: 			&meal.ID,
265: 			&meal.RecipeID,
266: 			&meal.RecipeName,
267: 			&meal.ScheduledDate,
268: 			&meal.MealSection,
269: 			&meal.Servings,
270: 			&meal.Calories,
271: 			&meal.ProteinG,
272: 			&meal.CarbsG,
273: 			&meal.FatG,
274: 			&meal.EstimatedCostCents,
275: 			&consumedInt,
276: 			&meal.ConsumedAt,
277: 		); err != nil {
278: 			return nil, err
279: 		}
280: 		meal.ScheduledDate = normalizeDateOnly(meal.ScheduledDate)
281: 		meal.IsConsumed = consumedInt == 1
282: 		meals = append(meals, meal)
283: 	}
284: 
285: 	return meals, rows.Err()
286: }
287: 
288: func normalizeDateOnly(value string) string {
289: 	value = strings.TrimSpace(value)
290: 	if value == "" {
291: 		return value
292: 	}
293: 	if len(value) >= 10 && value[4] == '-' && value[7] == '-' {
294: 		return value[:10]
295: 	}
296: 	for _, layout := range []string{time.RFC3339, "2006-01-02 15:04:05Z07:00", "2006-01-02 15:04:05", "2006-01-02"} {
297: 		if t, err := time.Parse(layout, value); err == nil {
298: 			return t.Format("2006-01-02")
299: 		}
300: 	}
301: 	return value
302: }

21: func (r *PantryRepository) ListByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
22: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
23: 		ctx,
24: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, p.unit, f.fdc_id, f.description, COALESCE(f.food_class, '')
25: 		 FROM pantry_items p
26: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
27: 		 WHERE p.user_id = ?
28: 		 ORDER BY f.description ASC`,
29: 		userID,
30: 	)
31: 	if err != nil {
32: 		return nil, err
33: 	}
34: 	defer rows.Close()
35: 
36: 	items := make([]dto.PantryItemResponse, 0)
37: 	for rows.Next() {
38: 		var item dto.PantryItemResponse
39: 		if err := rows.Scan(&item.ID, &item.Quantity, &item.Unit, &item.Food.FDCID, &item.Food.Description, &item.Food.FoodClass); err != nil {
40: 			return nil, err
41: 		}
42: 		items = append(items, item)
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	return items, rows.Err()
46: }
47: 
48: func (r *PantryRepository) Upsert(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PantryItemRequest) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
49: 	itemID, err := id.New("pnt")
50: 	if err != nil {
51: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
52: 	}
53: 
54: 	unit := strings.TrimSpace(req.Unit)
55: 	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
56: 		ctx,
57: 		`INSERT INTO pantry_items (id, user_id, fdc_id, quantity, unit)
58: 		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
59: 		 ON CONFLICT(user_id, fdc_id, unit) DO UPDATE SET
60: 			 quantity = pantry_items.quantity + excluded.quantity,
61: 			 updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`,
62: 		itemID,
63: 		userID,
64: 		req.FDCID,
65: 		req.Quantity,
66: 		unit,
67: 	)
68: 	if err != nil {
69: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	return r.GetByUserAndFood(ctx, userID, req.FDCID, unit)
73: }
74: 
75: func (r *PantryRepository) GetByUserAndFood(ctx context.Context, userID string, fdcID int64, unit string) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
76: 	var item dto.PantryItemResponse
77: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
78: 		ctx,
79: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, p.unit, f.fdc_id, f.description, COALESCE(f.food_class, '')
80: 		 FROM pantry_items p
81: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
82: 		 WHERE p.user_id = ? AND p.fdc_id = ? AND p.unit = ?`,
83: 		userID,
84: 		fdcID,
85: 		unit,
86: 	).Scan(&item.ID, &item.Quantity, &item.Unit, &item.Food.FDCID, &item.Food.Description, &item.Food.FoodClass)
87: 	return item, err
88: }

90: func (r *PantryRepository) PatchQuantity(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string, delta float64) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
91: 	_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
92: 		ctx,
93: 		`UPDATE pantry_items
94: 		 SET quantity = CASE
95: 			 WHEN quantity + ? < 0 THEN 0
96: 			 ELSE quantity + ?
97: 		 END,
98: 		 updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
99: 		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
100: 		delta,
101: 		delta,
102: 		itemID,
103: 		userID,
104: 	)
105: 	if err != nil {
106: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	return r.GetByID(ctx, userID, itemID)
110: }

115: 		ctx,
116: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, p.unit, f.fdc_id, f.description, COALESCE(f.food_class, '')
117: 		 FROM pantry_items p
118: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
119: 		 WHERE p.id = ? AND p.user_id = ?`,
120: 		itemID,
121: 		userID,
122: 	).Scan(&item.ID, &item.Quantity, &item.Unit, &item.Food.FDCID, &item.Food.Description, &item.Food.FoodClass)
123: 	return item, err
124: }
125: 
126: func (r *PantryRepository) Delete(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string) error {
127: 	result, err := r.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pantry_items WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`, itemID, userID)
128: 	if err != nil {
129: 		return err
130: 	}
131: 	affected, err := result.RowsAffected()
132: 	if err != nil {
133: 		return err
134: 	}
135: 	if affected == 0 {
136: 		return sql.ErrNoRows
137: 	}
138: 	return nil
139: }
140: 
141: func (r *PantryRepository) Exists(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string) (bool, error) {
142: 	_, err := r.GetByID(ctx, userID, itemID)
143: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
144: 		return false, nil
145: 	}
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return false, err
148: 	}
149: 	return true, nil
150: }

1: package middleware
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"encoding/json"
5: 	"net/http"
6: 	"strings"
7: 
8: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/auth"
9: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
10: )
11: 
12: func AuthRequired(tokens *auth.TokenManager, users *repositories.UserRepository) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
13: 	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
14: 		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
15: 			authHeader := strings.TrimSpace(r.Header.Get("Authorization"))
16: 			token := strings.TrimPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ")
17: 			if token == authHeader || token == "" {
18: 				writeAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing bearer token")
19: 				return
20: 			}
21: 
22: 			claims, err := tokens.ParseToken(token)
23: 			if err != nil {
24: 				writeAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "invalid token")
25: 				return
26: 			}
27: 
28: 			exists, err := users.ExistsByID(r.Context(), claims.UserID)
29: 			if err != nil || !exists {
30: 				writeAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "invalid token user")
31: 				return
32: 			}
33: 
34: 			ctx := WithUserID(r.Context(), claims.UserID)
35: 			next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
36: 		})
37: 	}
38: }
39: 
40: func writeAPIError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, code, message string) {
41: 	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
42: 	w.WriteHeader(status)
43: 	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]any{
44: 		"error": map[string]string{
45: 			"code":    code,
46: 			"message": message,
47: 		},
48: 	})
49: }

12: type PlanHandler struct {

14: }

16: func NewPlanHandler(plans *services.PlanService) *PlanHandler {
17: 	return &PlanHandler{plans: plans}
18: }
19: 
20: func (h *PlanHandler) CreateProposal(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
21: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
22: 	if !ok {
23: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")
24: 		return
25: 	}
26: 
27: 	var req dto.PlanProposalRequest
28: 	if err := ReadJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
29: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "bad_request", "invalid JSON payload")
30: 		return
31: 	}
32: 
33: 	res, err := h.plans.CreateProposal(r.Context(), userID, req)
34: 	if err != nil {
35: 		switch {
36: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidPlanType),
37: 			errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidStartDate),
38: 			errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidEndDate),
39: 			errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidPlanMeals),
40: 			errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidMealSection):
41: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "validation_error", err.Error())
42: 		default:
43: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to create plan proposal")
44: 		}
45: 		return
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, res)
49: }
50: 
51: func (h *PlanHandler) AcceptProposal(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
52: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
53: 	if !ok {
54: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")
55: 		return
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	res, err := h.plans.AcceptProposal(r.Context(), userID, r.PathValue("id"))
59: 	if err != nil {
60: 		switch {
61: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidPlanStatus):
62: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "not_found", err.Error())
63: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrPlanNotProposal):
64: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusConflict, "conflict", err.Error())
65: 		default:
66: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to accept plan")
67: 		}
68: 		return
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, res)
72: }
73:

75: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
76: 	if !ok {
77: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")

85: 	}
86:

94: 		default:
95: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to decline plan")
96: 		}
97: 		return
98: 	}

100: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, res)
101: }

1: package services
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"errors"
7: 	"strings"
8: 
9: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
10: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
11: )
12: 
13: var (
14: 	ErrInvalidSearchQuery = errors.New("query parameter q is required")
15: 	ErrInvalidPantryFood  = errors.New("fdcId must reference an existing USDA food")
16: 	ErrInvalidQuantity    = errors.New("quantity must be greater than 0")
17: 	ErrInvalidUnit        = errors.New("unit is required")
18: 	ErrPantryItemNotFound = errors.New("pantry item not found")
19: )
20: 
21: type PantryService struct {
22: 	foods  *repositories.FoodRepository
23: 	pantry *repositories.PantryRepository
24: }
25: 
26: func NewPantryService(foods *repositories.FoodRepository, pantry *repositories.PantryRepository) *PantryService {
27: 	return &PantryService{foods: foods, pantry: pantry}
28: }
29: 
30: func (s *PantryService) SearchFoods(ctx context.Context, query string) ([]dto.FoodSearchItem, error) {
31: 	if strings.TrimSpace(query) == "" {
32: 		return nil, ErrInvalidSearchQuery
33: 	}
34: 	return s.foods.SearchFoods(ctx, query, 20)
35: }
36: 
37: func (s *PantryService) ListPantryItems(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
38: 	return s.pantry.ListByUserID(ctx, userID)
39: }
40: 
41: func (s *PantryService) AddPantryItem(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PantryItemRequest) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
42: 	if req.Quantity <= 0 {
43: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, ErrInvalidQuantity
44: 	}
45: 	if strings.TrimSpace(req.Unit) == "" {
46: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, ErrInvalidUnit
47: 	}
48: 	exists, err := s.foods.ExistsByFDCID(ctx, req.FDCID)
49: 	if err != nil {
50: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
51: 	}
52: 	if !exists {
53: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, ErrInvalidPantryFood
54: 	}
55: 	return s.pantry.Upsert(ctx, userID, req)
56: }

parts.text:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me implement the consumption endpoint. Here's my plan:

1. **DTOs** - Add `ConsumeMealResponse` and `ConsumedIngredientInfo` to `types.go`
2. **ConsumeService** - Create `consume_service.go` with:
   - Load the plan meal by ID, verify it belongs to the requesting user
   - Get recipe ingredients via recipe repository
   - For each ingredient, find matching pantry item, deduct quantity
   - Clamp at zero
   - Log to consumption_log
   - Mark plan_meal as consumed
3. **ConsumeHandler** - Create `consume_handler.go`
4. **Wire into router and app**

Let me think about the service design. I need:
- Access to plan repository (to get the plan meal, verify user ownership)
- Access to recipe repository (to get recipe ingredients)
- Access to pantry repository (to find and update pantry items)

Wait, looking at the plan repository, `GetPlanByID` takes a userID and planID and filters by user_id. But to get a specific plan_meal, I need to load the plan and find the meal in it.

Let me think about the most efficient approach:

1. The handler receives `{id}` which is a plan_meal ID.
2. I need to verify the plan_meal belongs to the user.
3. Load the recipe ingredients if `recipe_id` is set.
4. For each ingredient, find the matching pantry item for this user.
5. Deduct the quantity.

I think I should create a new method on `PlanRepository` to get a plan_meal by its ID (which includes the plan's user_id check through a join).

Actually, I could add a method to get a plan meal directly, joining with the plan to check user_id. Let me think about how to structure this.

The simplest approach:
1. Plan repository: add `GetPlanMealByID(ctx, userID, mealID)` that joins plan_meals with meal_plans to verify ownership.
2. The service will:
   - Load the plan meal
   - Check if it's already consumed
   - Load recipe ingredients
   - For each ingredient, find the pantry item for this user with matching fdc_id
   - Deduct from pantry (using existing PatchQuantity which already clamps at zero)
   - Log to consumption_log
   - Mark plan_meal as consumed

Wait, but `PatchQuantity` in the pantry repo works by pantry item ID, not by fdc_id. I need to find the pantry item by user_id and fdc_id. Let me check if there's a way to do that.

Looking at the pantry repository, `GetByUserAndFood` takes userID, fdcID, unit. But I need to deduct from the pantry item that matches the ingredient's fdc_id. The issue is that a user could have the same fdc_id with different units. I should match by fdc_id and unit from the recipe ingredient.

Let me think about this more carefully. The recipe_ingredient has fdc_id, quantity, and unit. The pantry item also has fdc_id, quantity, and unit. So I need to:
1. Find the pantry item with matching user_id, fdc_id, and unit
2. Deduct the ingredient's quantity from it

I'll need to add a method to the pantry repository that deducts by fdc_id and unit (not by pantry item id). Or I can query for the pantry item first, then use PatchQuantity or a direct update.

Actually, I think it's cleaner to add a `DeductByFood` method to the pantry repository that does:
```sql
UPDATE pantry_items 
SET quantity = CASE WHEN quantity - ? < 0 THEN 0 ELSE quantity - ? END,
    updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE user_id = ? AND fdc_id = ? AND unit = ?
```

And also returns the before/after quantities for logging.

But this might match multiple rows if there are multiple pantry items with same fdc_id and unit... wait, the UNIQUE constraint is `(user_id, fdc_id, unit)` so it's at most one.

Let me design the implementation now:

### PlanRepository additions:
- `GetPlanMealByID(ctx, userID, mealID string) (StoredPlanMeal, error)` - joins plan_meals with meal_plans to verify ownership

### PantryRepository additions:
- `DeductByFood(ctx, userID string, fdcID int64, unit string, quantity float64) (PantryDeduction, error)` - deducts and returns before/after
- Need a new struct `PantryDeduction` with before, after, pantryItemID

### New file: `consumption_log_repository.go` or just a method on a new repo
- `Insert(ctx, planMealID, userID, fdcID, pantryItemID, quantityDeducted, unit string, beforeQty, afterQty float64, warning string) error`

Actually, looking at the consumption_log table, I can see it has all the fields I need. Let me create a ConsumptionLogRepository.

### DTOs:
- `ConsumeMealResponse` with consumed items and warnings
- `ConsumedItemInfo` for each item

### Service:
- `ConsumeService` with `ConsumeMeal(ctx, userID, mealID string) (dto.ConsumeMealResponse, error)`

Let me now implement all this. I'll create:

1. Add DTOs to `types.go`
2. Create `consumption_log_repository.go`
3. Add `GetPlanMealByID` to `plan_repository.go`
4. Add `DeductByFood` to `pantry_repository.go`
5. Create `consume_service.go`
6. Create `consume_handler.go`
7. Update `router.go`
8. Update `app.go`

Let me look at how the ID generation works to use it for consumption_log entries.

input.newString:
func (r *PlanRepository) GetPlanMealByID(ctx context.Context, userID, mealID string) (StoredPlanMeal, error) {
	var meal StoredPlanMeal
	var consumedInt int
	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
		ctx,
		`SELECT pm.id, COALESCE(pm.recipe_id, ''), pm.recipe_name, pm.scheduled_date, pm.meal_section,
		        pm.servings, COALESCE(pm.kcal, 0), COALESCE(pm.protein_g, 0), COALESCE(pm.carbs_g, 0),
		        COALESCE(pm.fat_g, 0), pm.estimated_cost_cents, pm.is_consumed, COALESCE(pm.consumed_at, '')
		 FROM plan_meals pm
		 JOIN meal_plans mp ON mp.id = pm.meal_plan_id
		 WHERE pm.id = ? AND mp.user_id = ?`,
		mealID,
		userID,
	).Scan(
		&meal.ID,
		&meal.RecipeID,
		&meal.RecipeName,
		&meal.ScheduledDate,
		&meal.MealSection,
		&meal.Servings,
		&meal.Calories,
		&meal.ProteinG,
		&meal.CarbsG,
		&meal.FatG,
		&meal.EstimatedCostCents,
		&consumedInt,
		&meal.ConsumedAt,
	)
	if err != nil {
		return StoredPlanMeal{}, err
	}
	meal.ScheduledDate = normalizeDateOnly(meal.ScheduledDate)
	meal.IsConsumed = consumedInt == 1
	return meal, nil
}

func (r *PlanRepository) MarkMealConsumed(ctx context.Context, mealID string) error {
	_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
		ctx,
		`UPDATE plan_meals SET is_consumed = 1, consumed_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
		mealID,
	)
	return err
}

func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlan(ctx context.Context, planID string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {

+type PantryDeduction struct {
+	PantryItemID string
+	BeforeQty    float64
+	AfterQty     float64
+	FoodDesc     string
+}
+
+func (r *PantryRepository) DeductByFood(ctx context.Context, userID string, fdcID int64, unit string, quantity float64) (PantryDeduction, error) {
+	var d PantryDeduction
+	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
+		ctx,
+		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, f.description
+		 FROM pantry_items p
+		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
+		 WHERE p.user_id = ? AND p.fdc_id = ? AND p.unit = ?`,
+		userID,
+		fdcID,
+		unit,
+	).Scan(&d.PantryItemID, &d.BeforeQty, &d.FoodDesc)
+	if err != nil {
+		return PantryDeduction{}, err
+	}
+
+	newQty := d.BeforeQty - quantity
+	if newQty < 0 {
+		newQty = 0
+	}
+
+	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
+		ctx,
+		`UPDATE pantry_items SET quantity = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
+		newQty,
+		d.PantryItemID,
+	)
+	if err != nil {
+		return PantryDeduction{}, err
+	}
+
+	d.AfterQty = newQty
+	return d, nil
+}
+
 func (r *PantryRepository) Exists(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string) (bool, error) {
 	_, err := r.GetByID(ctx, userID, itemID)
 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
 		return false, nil

1. Load plan meal by ID, verify user ownership

5. Deduct ingredient quantity from pantry item
6. Log to consumption_log
7. If no matching pantry item, still log with a warning
8. Mark plan_meal as consumed
9. Return response with consumed items and warnings

The task says "Load the recipe (if linked via recipe_id) to get ingredient fdc_id + quantities" and "For each ingredient, find the user's matching pantry item and deduct the quantity". This implies that the deduction is based on the recipe's ingredient quantities, not anything else.

But wait - there's a complication. A recipe's ingredient quantities are associated with the recipe itself (not scaled by servings). The plan_meal has a `servings` field. So if a recipe is for 2 servings and the plan meal says 1 serving, we should scale down. But actually, the recipe's ingredients are stored with their actual quantities in recipe_ingredients, and the recipe has its own servings count. The plan_meal's servings is the number of servings of that recipe being consumed.

Let me look at the recipe data again. The recipe_ingredients table has `quantity` per ingredient. I need to scale the ingredient quantities by the plan_meal's servings relative to the recipe's servings.

Wait, let me reconsider. The recipe_ingredients stores absolute quantities, not per-serving. And the recipe has its own `servings` column. The plan_meal also has `servings`. So I should calculate: ingredient_quantity = (recipe_ingredient.quantity / recipe.servings) * plan_meal.servings.

But that requires loading the recipe's servings count as well.

Actually, let me look at the recipe table schema again:
```
CREATE TABLE recipes (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    meal_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    servings INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (servings > 0),
    ...
);
```

And recipe_ingredients:
```
CREATE TABLE recipe_ingredients (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    recipe_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    fdc_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity >= 0),
    unit TEXT NOT NULL,
    ...
    UNIQUE (recipe_id, fdc_id, unit)
);
```

So ingredient quantity in recipe_ingredients is the total for the recipe, and the recipe has servings. Plan meal servings tells us how many servings we're consuming.

Scale factor = plan_meal.servings / recipe.servings

So ingredient deduction = recipe_ingredient.quantity * (plan_meal.servings / recipe.servings)

But I need the recipe's serving count. Let me load the recipe_id and servings from the recipe table. I can create a method on RecipeRepository to get just the servings count, or I can load the full recipe.

Actually, the simplest approach: load the recipe by ID (which already exists) and get its servings. Then compute the scale factor.

Wait, but I already have a `GetPlanMealByID` that returns the plan meal with its servings and recipe_id. The recipe repository already has `GetByID` which returns the full recipe with its servings and ingredients.

Let me just use the existing `RecipeService` to get the recipe. But that's a service, and I'm creating another service. Services can depend on each other in Go - that's fine.

Actually, let me reconsider the architecture. The ConsumeService needs:
- PlanRepository (to get plan meal, mark it consumed)
- RecipeRepository (to get recipe ingredients)
- PantryRepository (to deduct from pantry)
- ConsumptionLogRepository (to log)

I already have a PlanService and RecipeService, but the ConsumeService can directly use the repositories for cleaner implementation.

Let me think about what data I need from the recipe:
1. The recipe's servings count (to scale ingredients)
2. The ingredients list (fdc_id, quantity, unit, description)

The `RecipeRepository.GetByID` already returns all this. I can use that.

But I also need the ingredient descriptions (for the response). The recipe repository loads them via the JOIN with usda_foods.

So my ConsumeService will:
1. Load the plan meal via PlanRepository.GetPlanMealByID
2. Check if already consumed - if so, error
3. If recipe_id is set, load the recipe via RecipeRepository.GetByID
4. If no recipe_id (manual meal), still mark consumed but with empty items
5. For each ingredient, compute scaled quantity, find pantry item, deduct, log
6. Mark meal as consumed
7. Return response

Let me write the service now.

Actually, the recipe ingredients in the recipe repository use `RecipeIngredientResponse` which has FDCID, Description, Quantity, Unit. That's exactly what I need.

For the scale factor: `scale = planMeal.Servings / recipe.Servings`

Let me create the service.

12: )
13: 
14: var (
15: 	ErrPlanMealNotFound    = errors.New("plan meal not found")
16: 	ErrMealAlreadyConsumed = errors.New("meal has already been consumed")
17: )
18: 
19: type ConsumeService struct {
20: 	plans    *repositories.PlanRepository
21: 	recipes  *repositories.RecipeRepository
22: 	pantry   *repositories.PantryRepository
23: 	consumption *repositories.ConsumptionLogRepository
24: }
25: 
26: func NewConsumeService(plans *repositories.PlanRepository, recipes *repositories.RecipeRepository, pantry *repositories.PantryRepository, consumption *repositories.ConsumptionLogRepository) *ConsumeService {
27: 	return &ConsumeService{plans: plans, recipes: recipes, pantry: pantry, consumption: consumption}
28: }
29: 
30: func (s *ConsumeService) ConsumeMeal(ctx context.Context, userID, mealID string) (dto.ConsumeMealResponse, error) {
31: 	meal, err := s.plans.GetPlanMealByID(ctx, userID, mealID)
32: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
33: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, ErrPlanMealNotFound
34: 	}
35: 	if err != nil {
36: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, err
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	if meal.IsConsumed {
40: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, ErrMealAlreadyConsumed
41: 	}
42: 
43: 	var items []dto.ConsumedItemInfo
44: 
45: 	if meal.RecipeID != "" {
46: 		recipe, err := s.recipes.GetByID(ctx, meal.RecipeID)
47: 		if err != nil {
48: 			return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("load recipe: %w", err)
49: 		}
50: 
51: 		scale := meal.Servings / float64(recipe.Servings)
52: 
53: 		for _, ing := range recipe.Ingredients {
54: 			deductQty := ing.Quantity * scale
55: 
56: 			info := s.deductIngredient(ctx, userID, mealID, ing.FDCID, ing.Description, deductQty, ing.Unit)
57: 			items = append(items, info)
58: 		}
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	if err := s.plans.MarkMealConsumed(ctx, mealID); err != nil {
62: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("mark meal consumed: %w", err)
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
66: 
67: 	return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{
68: 		MealID:    mealID,
69: 		Consumed:  true,
70: 		ConsumedAt: now,
71: 		Items:     items,
72: 	}, nil
73: }
74: 
75: func (s *ConsumeService) deductIngredient(ctx context.Context, userID, mealID string, fdcID int64, description string, quantity float64, unit string) dto.ConsumedItemInfo {
76: 	info := dto.ConsumedItemInfo{
77: 		FDCID:           fdcID,
78: 		Description:     description,
79: 		QuantityDeducted: quantity,
80: 		Unit:            unit,
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	deduction, err := s.pantry.DeductByFood(ctx, userID, fdcID, unit, quantity)
84: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
85: 		info.BeforeQuantity = 0
86: 		info.AfterQuantity = 0
87: 		info.QuantityDeducted = 0
88: 		info.Warning = fmt.Sprintf("no pantry item found for %s (%s)", description, unit)
89: 
90: 		_ = s.consumption.Insert(ctx, repositories.ConsumptionLogEntry{
91: 			PlanMealID:       mealID,
92: 			UserID:           userID,
93: 			FDCID:            fdcID,
94: 			QuantityDeducted: 0,
95: 			Unit:             unit,
96: 			Warning:          info.Warning,
97: 		})
98: 		return info
99: 	}
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		info.Warning = fmt.Sprintf("failed to deduct: %v", err)
102: 		return info
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	info.PantryItemID = deduction.PantryItemID
106: 	info.BeforeQuantity = deduction.BeforeQty
107: 	info.AfterQuantity = deduction.AfterQty
108: 
109: 	if deduction.BeforeQty < quantity {
110: 		info.Warning = fmt.Sprintf("insufficient quantity: had %.2f %s, needed %.2f %s", deduction.BeforeQty, unit, quantity, unit)
111: 		info.QuantityDeducted = deduction.BeforeQty - deduction.AfterQty
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	_ = s.consumption.Insert(ctx, repositories.ConsumptionLogEntry{
115: 		PlanMealID:       mealID,
116: 		UserID:           userID,
117: 		FDCID:            fdcID,
118: 		PantryItemID:     deduction.PantryItemID,
119: 		QuantityDeducted: info.QuantityDeducted,
120: 		Unit:             unit,
121: 		BeforeQuantity:   deduction.BeforeQty,
122: 		AfterQuantity:    deduction.AfterQty,
123: 		Warning:          info.Warning,
124: 	})
125: 
126: 	return info
127: }

parts.text:
Step 1 is done. Here's a summary of all changes:

### Files Created
- **`backend/internal/services/consume_service.go`** — `ConsumeService` with `ConsumeMeal()` that loads the plan meal, verifies ownership, scales recipe ingredients by servings ratio, deducts from pantry via `DeductByFood`, logs each deduction to `consumption_log`, and marks the meal as consumed
- **`backend/internal/repositories/consumption_log_repository.go`** — `ConsumptionLogRepository` with `Insert()` for audit trail entries
- **`backend/internal/transport/http/handlers/consume_handler.go`** — `ConsumeHandler` with `ConsumeMeal()` endpoint handler

### Files Modified
- **`backend/internal/transport/http/dto/types.go`** — Added `ConsumedItemInfo` and `ConsumeMealResponse` DTOs
- **`backend/internal/repositories/plan_repository.go`** — Added `GetPlanMealByID()` (joins with `meal_plans` to verify user ownership) and `MarkMealConsumed()`
- **`backend/internal/repositories/pantry_repository.go`** — Added `DeductByFood()` (looks up by `user_id`/`fdc_id`/`unit`, deducts with clamp-at-zero, returns before/after quantities + food description)
- **`backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go`** — Added `Consume` to `Handlers` struct and route `POST /plan-meals/{id}/consume`
- **`backend/internal/app/app.go`** — Wired `ConsumptionLogRepository`, `ConsumeService`, and `ConsumeHandler`

### Key Behaviors
- **Ownership check**: `GetPlanMealByID` joins `plan_meals` with `meal_plans` and filters by `user_id`
- **Already-consumed**: Returns 409 if `is_consumed = 1`
- **Ingredient scaling**: Scales recipe ingredient quantities by `plan_meal.servings / recipe.servings`
- **Pantry deduction**: Matches by `(user_id, fdc_id, unit)`; clamps at zero; warns on insufficient stock
- **Missing pantry items**: Logs with `quantity_deducted=0` and a warning; doesn't block consumption
- **Audit trail**: Every deduction (or attempt) is logged in `consumption_log` with before/after quantities

Index: backend/internal/repositories/chat_repository.go
===================================================================
--- backend/internal/repositories/chat_repository.go	
+++ backend/internal/repositories/chat_repository.go	
@@ -0,0 +1,97 @@
+package repositories
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"database/sql"
+	"time"
+
+	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/id"
+)
+
+type ChatRepository struct {
+	db *sql.DB
+}
+
+func NewChatRepository(db *sql.DB) *ChatRepository {
+	return &ChatRepository{db: db}
+}
+
+type StoredChatMessage struct {
+	ID        string
+	UserID    string
+	Role      string
+	Action    string
+	Content   string
+	CreatedAt string
+}
+
+func (r *ChatRepository) InsertUserMessage(ctx context.Context, userID, message, action string) (StoredChatMessage, error) {
+	msgID, err := id.New("msg")
+	if err != nil {
+		return StoredChatMessage{}, err
+	}
+
+	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
+
+	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
+		ctx,
+		`INSERT INTO chat_messages (id, user_id, role, action, content, metadata_json, created_at)
+		 VALUES (?, ?, 'user', NULLIF(?, ''), ?, '{}', ?)`,
+		msgID,
+		userID,
+		action,
+		message,
+		now,
+	)
+	if err != nil {
+		return StoredChatMessage{}, err
+	}
+
+	return StoredChatMessage{
+		ID:        msgID,
+		UserID:    userID,
+		Role:      "user",
+		Action:    action,
+		Content:   message,
+		CreatedAt: now,
+	}, nil
+}
+
+func (r *ChatRepository) ListRecent(ctx context.Context, userID string, limit int) ([]StoredChatMessage, error) {
+	if limit <= 0 || limit > 100 {
+		limit = 50
+	}
+
+	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
+		ctx,
+		`SELECT id, user_id, role, COALESCE(action, ''), content, created_at
+		 FROM chat_messages
+		 WHERE user_id = ?
+		 ORDER BY created_at DESC
+		 LIMIT ?`,
+		userID,
+		limit,
+	)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	defer rows.Close()
+
+	messages := make([]StoredChatMessage, 0, limit)
+	for rows.Next() {
+		var msg StoredChatMessage
+		if err := rows.Scan(&msg.ID, &msg.UserID, &msg.Role, &msg.Action, &msg.Content, &msg.CreatedAt); err != nil {
+			return nil, err
+		}
+		messages = append(messages, msg)
+	}
+	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	for i, j := 0, len(messages)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
+		messages[i], messages[j] = messages[j], messages[i]
+	}
+
+	return messages, nil
+}

parts.text:
Also, I'm importing `dto` in the chat_service.go, let me check if dto is used there. Yes, it is: the service accepts and returns DTO types. Good.

Let me also check the ChatRepository's `StoredChatMessage` fields - `CreatedAt` is a `string`. When I insert, I pass `now` as the `created_at` value directly. Let me make sure that's ok with the SQLite schema which has `created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`. I'm explicitly setting created_at, so it should be fine.

But wait, there's a subtle issue. The `ListRecent` query selects `created_at` from the database, which will be in whatever format SQLite returns it (which could be the RFC3339 string I inserted, or the default timestamp format). Since I'm explicitly inserting in RFC3339, the SELECT will return that format. For messages inserted with the DEFAULT (by other code), it would be in SQLite's default format. But since I control all inserts, this should be consistent.

Step 2 is done. Here's a summary:

### Files Created
- **`backend/internal/services/chat_service.go`** — `ChatService` with `SendMessage()` (stores user message, returns it) and `GetHistory()` (returns recent messages with optional limit)
- **`backend/internal/repositories/chat_repository.go`** — `ChatRepository` with `InsertUserMessage()` (stores with `role='user'`, explicit RFC3339 timestamp) and `ListRecent()` (DESC query then reversed to ASC order, capped at 100)
- **`backend/internal/transport/http/handlers/chat_handler.go`** — `ChatHandler` with `SendMessage()` and `GetHistory()`

### Files Modified
- **`backend/internal/transport/http/dto/types.go`** — Added `ChatSendRequest`, `ChatMessageResponse`, `ChatHistoryResponse`
- **`backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go`** — Added `Chat` to `Handlers` struct, routes `POST /chat` and `GET /chat`
- **`backend/internal/app/app.go`** — Wired `ChatRepository`, `ChatService`, `ChatHandler`

### Endpoint behavior
- **`POST /chat`** (auth required) — Accepts `{ message, action? }`, validates message is non-empty, stores with `role='user'`, returns 201 with the stored message
- **`GET /chat?limit=50`** (auth required) — Returns messages in chronological ASC order, default limit 50, max 100, returns `{ messages: [...] }`

40: func (s *GenerateService) tryGemini(ctx context.Context, userID string, periodType string, userMessage string) (GeneratePlanResult, error) {
41: 	promptReq := buildPromptContext(ctx, s.profile, s.pantry, userID, periodType, userMessage)
42: 	promptReq.ResponseContract = responseContract(periodType)
43:

72: 	}

82: func buildPromptContext(ctx context.Context, profile *ProfileService, pantry *PantryService, userID string, periodType string, userMessage string) ai.PromptRequest {
83: 	req := ai.PromptRequest{
84: 		UserRequest: userMessage,
85: 		StartDate:   time.Now().UTC().Format("2006-01-02"),
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	profileResp, err := profile.GetProfile(ctx, userID)
89: 	if err == nil {
90: 		prefs := profileResp.Preferences
91: 		var prefParts []string
92: 		prefParts = append(prefParts, fmt.Sprintf("Diet type: %s", prefs.DietType))
93: 		if len(prefs.Allergies) > 0 {
94: 			prefParts = append(prefParts, fmt.Sprintf("Allergies: %s", strings.Join(prefs.Allergies, ", ")))
95: 		}
96: 		if len(prefs.Dislikes) > 0 {
97: 			prefParts = append(prefParts, fmt.Sprintf("Dislikes: %s", strings.Join(prefs.Dislikes, ", ")))
98: 		}
99: 		if len(prefs.Likes) > 0 {
100: 			prefParts = append(prefParts, fmt.Sprintf("Likes: %s", strings.Join(prefs.Likes, ", ")))
101: 		}
102: 		if prefs.DailyCalorieTarget != nil {
103: 			prefParts = append(prefParts, fmt.Sprintf("Daily calorie target: %d", *prefs.DailyCalorieTarget))
104: 		}
105: 		req.PreferenceSummary = strings.Join(prefParts, "\n")
106: 
107: 		metrics := profileResp.Metrics
108: 		var metricParts []string
109: 		if metrics.HeightCM != nil {
110: 			metricParts = append(metricParts, fmt.Sprintf("Height: %.0f cm", *metrics.HeightCM))
111: 		}
112: 		if metrics.WeightKG != nil {
113: 			metricParts = append(metricParts, fmt.Sprintf("Weight: %.0f kg", *metrics.WeightKG))
114: 		}
115: 		if metrics.Age != nil {
116: 			metricParts = append(metricParts, fmt.Sprintf("Age: %d", *metrics.Age))
117: 		}
118: 		if metrics.Sex != nil {
119: 			metricParts = append(metricParts, fmt.Sprintf("Sex: %s", *metrics.Sex))
120: 		}
121: 		if metrics.ActivityLevel != nil {
122: 			metricParts = append(metricParts, fmt.Sprintf("Activity level: %s", *metrics.ActivityLevel))
123: 		}
124: 		if metrics.Goal != nil {
125: 			metricParts = append(metricParts, fmt.Sprintf("Goal: %s", *metrics.Goal))
126: 		}
127: 		req.BodyMetricsSummary = strings.Join(metricParts, "\n")
128: 
129: 		budget := profileResp.Budget
130: 		if budget.AmountCents > 0 {
131: 			req.BudgetTarget = fmt.Sprintf("Monthly budget: $%.2f %s", float64(budget.AmountCents)/100, budget.Currency)
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	pantryItems, err := pantry.ListPantryItems(ctx, userID)
136: 	if err == nil && len(pantryItems) > 0 {
137: 		snapshot := make([]string, 0, len(pantryItems))
138: 		for _, item := range pantryItems {
139: 			snapshot = append(snapshot, fmt.Sprintf("%s: %.1f %s", item.Food.Description, item.Quantity, item.Unit))
140: 		}
141: 		req.PantrySnapshot = snapshot
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	return req
145: }

147: func aiPromptTemplate(periodType string) ai.PromptTemplate {
148: 	switch periodType {
149: 	case "meal":
150: 		return ai.PromptTemplateSingleMeal
151: 	case "day":
152: 		return ai.PromptTemplateDayPlan
153: 	case "week":
154: 		return ai.PromptTemplateWeekPlan
155: 	case "month":
156: 		return ai.PromptTemplateMonthPlan
157: 	default:
158: 		return ai.PromptTemplateWeekPlan
159: 	}
160: }

163: 	return `{

169: }`
170: }

153: func (c *Client) Generate(ctx context.Context, req GenerateRequest) (GenerateResponse, error) {

195: 			return GenerateResponse{}, ctx.Err()
196: 		case <-time.After(c.retryBackoff * time.Duration(attempt+1)):
197: 		}
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	return GenerateResponse{}, lastErr
201: }
202: 
203: func (c *Client) doRequest(ctx context.Context, endpoint string, body []byte) (GenerateResponse, error) {
204: 	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, endpoint, bytes.NewReader(body))
205: 	if err != nil {
206: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
207: 	}
208: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
209: 
210: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
211: 	if err != nil {
212: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
213: 	}
214: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
215: 
216: 	responseBody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
217: 	if err != nil {
218: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	requestID := strings.TrimSpace(resp.Header.Get("x-request-id"))
222: 	if requestID == "" {
223: 		requestID = strings.TrimSpace(resp.Header.Get("x-goog-request-id"))
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	if resp.StatusCode >= http.StatusBadRequest {
227: 		return GenerateResponse{}, httpStatusError{
228: 			StatusCode: resp.StatusCode,
229: 			Body:       string(responseBody),
230: 			RequestID:  requestID,
231: 		}
232: 	}
233: 
234: 	var parsed geminiGenerateContentResponse
235: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(responseBody, &parsed); err != nil {
236: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	text := extractResponseText(parsed)
240: 	if text == "" {
241: 		return GenerateResponse{}, ErrUnexpectedResponse
242: 	}
243: 
244: 	return GenerateResponse{
245: 		Text:       text,
246: 		Model:      c.model,
247: 		StatusCode: resp.StatusCode,
248: 		RequestID:  requestID,
249: 	}, nil
250: }
251: 
252: func (c *Client) responseMIMEType(req GenerateRequest) string {
253: 	if strings.TrimSpace(req.ResponseMIMEType) != "" {
254: 		return req.ResponseMIMEType
255: 	}
256: 	return c.responseFormat
257: }
258: 
259: func extractResponseText(resp geminiGenerateContentResponse) string {
260: 	parts := make([]string, 0)
261: 	for _, candidate := range resp.Candidates {
262: 		for _, part := range candidate.Content.Parts {
263: 			text := strings.TrimSpace(part.Text)
264: 			if text != "" {
265: 				parts = append(parts, text)
266: 			}
267: 		}
268: 	}
269: 	return strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(parts, "\n"))
270: }
271: 
272: func isRetryable(err error) bool {
273: 	var statusErr httpStatusError
274: 	if errors.As(err, &statusErr) {
275: 		return statusErr.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests || statusErr.StatusCode >= http.StatusInternalServerError
276: 	}
277: 
278: 	var netErr net.Error
279: 	return errors.As(err, &netErr)
280: }
281: 
282: type httpStatusError struct {
283: 	StatusCode int
284: 	Body       string
285: 	RequestID  string
286: }
287: 
288: func (e httpStatusError) Error() string {
289: 	if e.RequestID != "" {
290: 		return fmt.Sprintf("gemini request failed with status %d (request_id=%s): %s", e.StatusCode, e.RequestID, strings.TrimSpace(e.Body))
291: 	}
292: 	return fmt.Sprintf("gemini request failed with status %d: %s", e.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(e.Body))
293: }

(End of file - total 293 lines)
</content>
